 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.1.1","title":"WORKFLOW: Specification Refinement with APR (V2 docs)","description":"# WORKFLOW: Specification Refinement with APR (V2 docs)

## Goal
Capture the APR-based iterative spec refinement workflow in a fully self-contained Bead so we do not need to reread README.md to run refinement rounds.

## Context
The repo uses APR (Automated Plan Reviser Pro) + Oracle (browser automation) to run multi-round reviews of the FCP2 docs with GPT Pro.

This workflow is **for documents**, not for connector binaries or the runtime platform. It exists to continuously harden and clarify:
- `FCP_Specification_V2.md` (authoritative)
- `docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md` (connector authoring + implementation guidance)
- `README.md` (overview + ops/dev UX)

## Scope
In scope:
- How to install and run APR rounds.
- How to include the implementation doc periodically.
- How to run remotely (Oracle serve mode) when the dev box has no browser.
- How to integrate APR feedback back into the docs in a disciplined way.

Out of scope:
- Any “backwards compatibility” plan with FCP1. FCP2 is a clean cutover.
- Installing Node/npm tooling as a requirement for the shipped platform.

## Why this matters
The spec is large and security-sensitive. Iterative AI review is a force multiplier, but only if:
- we can repeat it reliably,
- we can reproduce what changed and why,
- and we integrate feedback in a way that preserves the spec’s internal consistency.

## Baseline assumptions
- You have a GPT Pro account available for Oracle/APR.
- You can authenticate in a browser for the initial APR run.
- You can run the workflow either locally or with Oracle serve mode over Tailscale.

## Workflow configuration
APR workflow file: `.apr/workflows/fcp.yaml`

Expected config (informational):
- Documents:
  - `readme`: `README.md`
  - `spec`: `FCP_Specification_V2.md`
  - `implementation`: `docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md`

## Setup (one-time)
Install APR:
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/automated_plan_reviser_pro/main/install.sh" | bash
```

Install Oracle (browser automation):
```bash
npm install -g @steipete/oracle
```

Note: Oracle is tooling only; it is not part of the FCP2 runtime distribution.

## Running revision rounds
First round (requires manual ChatGPT login):
```bash
apr run 1 --login --wait
```

Subsequent rounds:
```bash
apr run 2
apr run 3 --include-impl  # include implementation doc every 3–4 rounds
```

Inspect status and output:
```bash
apr status
apr show 5
```

## Remote/SSH setup (Oracle “serve mode”)
Use this if your dev box is remote (SSH) and cannot open a local browser.

On your local machine (with browser):
```bash
oracle serve --port 9333 --token "your-secret-token"
```

On the remote server:
```bash
export ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST="100.x.x.x:9333"  # local machine’s Tailscale IP
export ORACLE_REMOTE_TOKEN="your-secret-token"

# test connection
oracle -p "test" -e browser -m "5.2 Thinking"

# now APR works normally
apr run 1
```

Important: use port `9333` (not `9222`) to avoid clashing with Chrome DevTools.

## Integration discipline (how to apply feedback)
When APR produces feedback for round N:

1) Prime the coding agent with full context (don’t assume partial memory):
- Read ALL of `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`.
- Read `FCP_Specification_V2.md` and `docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md`.

2) Integrate feedback carefully:
- Treat GPT output as suggestions; validate each claim against the spec’s goals.
- Prefer “mechanical enforcement” changes over editorial prose.
- Ensure changes do not introduce FCP1 compatibility shims.

3) Harmonize docs in this order:
- Update `README.md` first for the human-facing story.
- Update `docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md` to keep connector authoring aligned.
- Update `FCP_Specification_V2.md` last (authoritative) to ensure correctness.

4) Commit in logical groupings with clear messages.

## Useful APR commands
```bash
apr status          # check Oracle sessions
apr list            # list workflows
apr history         # show revision history
apr diff 4 5        # compare rounds 4 and 5
apr stats           # convergence analytics
apr integrate 5 -c  # copy integration prompt to clipboard
```

## Key files
- `FCP_Specification_V2.md` — main protocol specification
- `docs/fcp_model_connectors_rust.md` — Rust implementation guide
- `.apr/workflows/fcp.yaml` — workflow configuration
- `.apr/rounds/fcp/round_N.md` — GPT Pro output per round

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new contributor can run APR rounds without consulting README.md.
- The workflow notes explicitly keep FCP2 V2-only (no FCP1 compatibility work).
- The workflow documents remote mode and integration discipline.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.10","title":"[FCP2] Exactly-Once Semantics: OperationIntent + OperationReceipt + Idempotency","description":"# [FCP2] Exactly-Once Semantics: OperationIntent + OperationReceipt + Idempotency

## Goal
Provide exactly-once semantics for external side effects (where required) by combining:
- an idempotency key
- an OperationIntent pre-commit object
- an OperationReceipt object that is returned on retries

## Required behavior (spec-aligned)
- For Strict idempotency and for Risky/Dangerous operations:
  - MeshNode MUST store OperationIntent (Required retention) **before** executing external side effects.
  - OperationIntent SHOULD bind to the execution lease fencing token (`lease_seq`) when applicable.
  - OperationReceipt MUST reference the OperationIntent.
  - Retries with the same idempotency key must return the prior receipt without re-executing.

## Subtasks
- Define OperationIntent and OperationReceipt schemas (include refs/evidence fields; include optional upstream idempotency handle).
- Define storage/indexing model to look up receipts by idempotency key.
- Integrate with audit: emitting audit events on intent and receipt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests: retry returns same receipt without re-execution.
- Fault injection tests: crash between intent and receipt is handled safely (detect and reconcile incomplete intents).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.11","title":"[FCP2] Audit + Explainability: AuditEvent Chain, AuditHead, ZoneCheckpoint, DecisionReceipt","description":"# [FCP2] Audit + Explainability: AuditEvent Chain, AuditHead, ZoneCheckpoint, DecisionReceipt

## Goal
Make all security-relevant actions tamper-evident and explainable. Every denial MUST be traceable to specific evidence. Every security-critical action MUST be logged.

## Background and Motivation

### Why Audit Matters for FCP2
Audit is not optional logging—it is a **security invariant**. Without tamper-evident audit:
- Incident response is guesswork
- Compliance is unverifiable  
- "Why was this denied?" has no answer
- Compromised nodes can hide their actions

FCP2 audit is **hash-linked and quorum-signed by construction**, making it:
- Mechanically verifiable (any node can detect tampering)
- Fork-detectable (multiple heads for same epoch trigger alerts)
- Explainable (every denial includes evidence ObjectIds)

### Design Principles
1. **Tamper-evident by construction**: Hash-linked chains with monotonic seq
2. **Explainable by default**: DecisionReceipt includes evidence ObjectIds
3. **Efficient sync**: Monotonic seq enables O(1) freshness comparison
4. **Byzantine-resilient**: Quorum-signed heads under n/f model

## Normative Types (MUST implement per spec §23)

### AuditEvent (NORMATIVE)
```rust
/// Audit event (NORMATIVE)
/// 
/// Append-only, hash-linked audit chain per zone.
/// Each event links to its predecessor via `prev` and carries
/// a monotonic `seq` for O(1) freshness comparison.
pub struct AuditEvent {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// Correlation ID for request tracing (16 bytes)
    pub correlation_id: [u8; 16],
    /// Optional full trace context (NORMATIVE when present in InvokeRequest)
    /// Enables stitching mesh routing, connector execution, receipts, and audit together.
    pub trace_context: Option<TraceContext>,
    /// Event type (e.g., "secret.access", "capability.invoke", "elevation.granted")
    pub event_type: String,
    /// Actor who triggered the event
    pub actor: PrincipalId,
    /// Zone where event occurred
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    /// Connector ID (if applicable)
    pub connector_id: Option<ConnectorId>,
    /// Operation ID (if applicable)
    pub operation: Option<OperationId>,
    /// Capability token JTI (if applicable)
    pub capability_token_jti: Option<Uuid>,
    /// Request object ID (if applicable)
    pub request_object_id: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Result object ID (if applicable)
    pub result_object_id: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Previous event in chain (hash link) - enables tamper detection
    pub prev: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Monotonic chain sequence number (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison: seq_a > seq_b ⟹ a is fresher than b.
    pub seq: u64,
    /// When event occurred (Unix timestamp)
    pub occurred_at: u64,
    /// Signature by executing node
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### AuditHead (NORMATIVE)
```rust
/// Audit head checkpoint (NORMATIVE)
/// 
/// Quorum-signed checkpoint of the audit chain head.
/// Enables fast sync without full chain traversal.
pub struct AuditHead {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// Zone this head covers
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    /// Head event ObjectId (tip of the chain)
    pub head_event: ObjectId,
    /// Sequence number of head_event (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison without chain traversal.
    pub head_seq: u64,
    /// Fraction of expected nodes contributing (0.0-1.0)
    pub coverage: f64,
    /// Epoch this head was checkpointed
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,
    /// Quorum signatures from nodes (Byzantine-resilient)
    pub quorum_signatures: Vec<(TailscaleNodeId, Signature)>,
}
```

### ZoneCheckpoint (NORMATIVE)
```rust
/// Zone checkpoint for fast sync (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Quorum-signed checkpoint of zone state for efficient synchronization.
/// Acts as the single GC root (so reachability GC is well-defined).
/// Nodes can compare checkpoints to quickly determine staleness.
pub struct ZoneCheckpoint {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    
    // Enforceable heads (NORMATIVE):
    pub rev_head: ObjectId,           // Revocation chain head
    pub rev_seq: u64,                 // Revocation chain seq
    pub audit_head: ObjectId,         // Audit chain head
    pub audit_seq: u64,               // Audit chain seq
    
    // Policy/config heads (NORMATIVE):
    pub zone_definition_head: ObjectId,
    pub zone_policy_head: ObjectId,
    pub active_zone_key_manifest: ObjectId,
    
    /// Monotonic checkpoint sequence (NORMATIVE; per-zone)
    pub checkpoint_seq: u64,
    pub as_of_epoch: EpochId,
    /// Quorum-signed (Byzantine-resilient under n/f model)
    pub quorum_signatures: Vec<QuorumSignature>,
}
```

### DecisionReceipt (NORMATIVE)
```rust
/// Decision receipt for explainable allow/deny (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Content-addressed "why allowed/denied" record with stable reason_code
/// and evidence ObjectIds. This is what powers `fcp explain`.
pub struct DecisionReceipt {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// The request that was evaluated
    pub request_object_id: ObjectId,
    /// The decision (Allow or Deny)
    pub decision: Decision,
    /// Stable reason code for programmatic handling
    /// Examples: "capability.insufficient", "checkpoint.stale_frontier",
    /// "revocation.stale_frontier", "taint.public_input_dangerous"
    pub reason_code: String,
    /// Evidence ObjectIds that support this decision
    /// For denials: the missing capability, stale checkpoint, etc.
    /// For allows: the authorizing capability objects, approval tokens
    pub evidence: Vec<ObjectId>,
    /// Optional human-readable explanation
    pub explanation: Option<String>,
    /// Signature by evaluating node
    pub signature: Signature,
}

pub enum Decision {
    Allow,
    Deny,
}
```

### TraceContext (NORMATIVE when present)
```rust
/// Distributed trace context for end-to-end observability (NORMATIVE when present)
/// W3C Trace Context compatible.
pub struct TraceContext {
    /// 16-byte trace id (unique per logical request)
    pub trace_id: [u8; 16],
    /// 8-byte span id (unique per span within trace)
    pub span_id: [u8; 8],
    /// Sampling/flags (W3C Trace Context compatible)
    pub flags: u8,
}
```

## Required Audit Events (NORMATIVE)

The following events MUST be recorded:

| Event Type | Trigger | Required Fields |
|------------|---------|-----------------|
| `secret.access` | Secret reconstruction/use | secret_id, purpose, accessor |
| `capability.invoke` | Risky/Dangerous operation | capability_token_jti, operation |
| `elevation.granted` | Integrity elevation approved | approval_token_id, target_zone |
| `declassification.granted` | Confidentiality downgrade | from_zone, to_zone, object_ids |
| `zone.transition` | Data crosses zone boundary | from_zone, to_zone, object_id |
| `revocation.issued` | New revocation published | revocation_object_id, scope |
| `security.violation` | Policy violation detected | reason_code, evidence |

## Quorum Rules (NORMATIVE)

**Default:** CriticalWrite requires n - f signatures (see V2 spec §18).

- Nodes MUST refuse to advance AuditHead if quorum is not satisfied
- Exception: explicit degraded mode with owner acknowledgment
- Degraded mode MUST be logged and time-bounded

## Fork Detection (NORMATIVE)

Nodes discovering multiple heads for the same epoch MUST:
1. **Log the fork event** with both head ObjectIds
2. **Refuse to advance** until reconciled
3. **Alert owner** for manual resolution
4. **Emit security audit event** of type `audit.fork_detected`

## TraceContext Propagation (NORMATIVE when present)

When `InvokeRequest.trace_context` is present:
- It MUST be preserved through to `AuditEvent.trace_context`
- It MUST be preserved through to `OperationReceipt` (via header refs)
- This enables end-to-end distributed tracing across mesh routing, connector execution, receipts, and audit

## Acceptance Criteria

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Audit chain hash linking works (verify prev linkage)
- [ ] Monotonic seq invariants hold (seq always increases)
- [ ] Decision receipt includes evidence and stable reason code
- [ ] Fork detection identifies conflicting heads

### Property Tests
- [ ] Monotonic seq invariants under concurrent appends
- [ ] Hash chain integrity under random event insertion
- [ ] Quorum signature verification

### Integration Tests
- [ ] End-to-end trace context propagation
- [ ] DecisionReceipt enables `fcp explain` without guessing
- [ ] Fork detection triggers alert

### Benchmarks
- [ ] Audit event serialization
- [ ] Quorum signature verification
- [ ] Chain traversal vs seq comparison

## How This Serves Project Goals

1. **Security**: Tamper-evident audit makes compromise detection mechanical
2. **Explainability**: DecisionReceipt enables `fcp explain` to answer "why denied?"
3. **Compliance**: Hash-linked chain provides verifiable audit trail
4. **Operability**: Monotonic seq enables efficient sync and freshness checks
5. **Incident Response**: Evidence ObjectIds trace decisions to root cause

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.12","title":"[FCP2] Wire Protocol (Data Plane): FCPS Frames + SymbolEnvelope AEAD + Session MACs","description":"# [FCP2] Wire Protocol (Data Plane): FCPS Frames + SymbolEnvelope AEAD + Session MACs

## Goal
Implement the symbol-native data plane: FCPS frames carrying encrypted symbol payloads with session-layer authentication.

This issue owns **byte-accurate encoding/decoding** of:
- The authenticated FCPS datagram envelope (session_id/seq/mac)
- The inner FCPS frame (114-byte fixed header + symbol records)
- Per-symbol AEAD rules and AAD binding
- The SymbolEnvelope encryption/decryption logic

---

## On-Wire Datagram Envelope (NORMATIVE)

FCPS frames are carried inside an authenticated datagram envelope bound to a MeshSession:

```
FCPS_DATAGRAM (on-wire format):
  Bytes 0-15:   session_id [16]
  Bytes 16-23:  seq (u64 LE)
  Bytes 24-39:  mac [16] (Suite1/Suite2; truncated to 16 bytes)
  Bytes 40..:   fcps_frame_bytes (exact FCPS frame bytes per section 4.3)
```

### MAC Computation (NORMATIVE)

```rust
/// Compute session MAC for FCPS datagram (NORMATIVE)
fn compute_session_mac(
    k_mac_dir: &[u8; 32],
    session_id: &[u8; 16],
    direction: u8,  // 0x00 = i2r, 0x01 = r2i
    seq: u64,
    fcps_frame_bytes: &[u8],
) -> [u8; 16] {
    // Suite1: HMAC-SHA256 truncated to 16 bytes
    // Suite2: BLAKE3 keyed, truncated to 16 bytes
    let mut mac_input = Vec::new();
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(session_id);
    mac_input.push(direction);
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(&seq.to_le_bytes());
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(fcps_frame_bytes);

    let full_mac = blake3::keyed_hash(k_mac_dir, &mac_input);
    let mut truncated = [0u8; 16];
    truncated.copy_from_slice(&full_mac.as_bytes()[..16]);
    truncated
}
```

**Direction byte:**
- `0x00` for initiator to responder
- `0x01` for responder to initiator

### MTU Rule (NORMATIVE)

- `len(FCPS_DATAGRAM)` MUST be at most `max_datagram_bytes` (default: 1200)
- Applies to **full UDP payload** (envelope + frame), not just inner FCPS frame
- Implementations MUST reject datagrams exceeding configured maximum

---

## FCPS Frame Format (NORMATIVE; Fixed 114-byte Header)

```
FCPS FRAME FORMAT (Symbol-Native)

  Bytes 0-3:    Magic (0x46 0x43 0x50 0x53 = "FCPS")
  Bytes 4-5:    Version (u16 LE)
  Bytes 6-7:    Flags (u16 LE)
  Bytes 8-11:   Symbol Count (u32 LE)
  Bytes 12-15:  Total Payload Length (u32 LE)
  Bytes 16-47:  Object ID (32 bytes)
  Bytes 48-49:  Symbol Size (u16 LE, default 1024)
  Bytes 50-57:  Zone Key ID (8 bytes, for rotation)
  Bytes 58-89:  Zone ID hash (32 bytes, BLAKE3; see section 3.4)
  Bytes 90-97:  Epoch ID (u64 LE)
  Bytes 98-105: Sender Instance ID (u64 LE, reboot-safety for nonces)
  Bytes 106-113: Frame Seq (u64 LE, per-sender monotonic)
  Bytes 114+:   Symbol payloads (concatenated)

  Fixed header: 114 bytes
  Each symbol: 4 (ESI) + 2 (K) + N (data) + 16 (auth_tag)
               (nonce derived per algorithm; see derive_nonce12/24)

  NOTE (NORMATIVE): No separate checksum. Frame integrity is guaranteed by:
    - per-symbol AEAD auth tags (content-plane)
    - MeshSession MAC (data-plane, see section 4.2)
```

### Symbol Record Format (NORMATIVE)

Each symbol record within a frame:

```rust
/// Symbol record layout (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Each symbol record is:
/// - esi: u32 LE (4 bytes)     - Encoding Symbol ID
/// - k: u16 LE (2 bytes)       - Total source symbols
/// - data: [u8; N]             - Encrypted symbol payload
/// - auth_tag: [u8; 16]        - AEAD authentication tag
///
/// Total: 4 + 2 + symbol_size + 16 = 22 + symbol_size bytes per symbol
pub struct SymbolRecord {
    pub esi: u32,
    pub k: u16,
    pub data: Vec<u8>,
    pub auth_tag: [u8; 16],
}
```

---

## Frame Flags (NORMATIVE)

```rust
bitflags! {
    pub struct FrameFlags: u16 {
        const REQUIRES_ACK      = 0b0000_0000_0001;  // Requires acknowledgment
        const COMPRESSED        = 0b0000_0000_0010;  // zstd compressed
        const ENCRYPTED         = 0b0000_0000_0100;  // Zone-encrypted symbols
        const RESPONSE          = 0b0000_0000_1000;  // Response to request
        const ERROR             = 0b0000_0001_0000;  // Error response
        const STREAMING         = 0b0000_0010_0000;  // Part of stream
        const STREAM_END        = 0b0000_0100_0000;  // Final frame in stream
        const HAS_CAP_TOKEN     = 0b0000_1000_0000;  // Contains capability token
        const ZONE_CROSSING     = 0b0001_0000_0000;  // Zone transition
        const PRIORITY          = 0b0010_0000_0000;  // High priority
        const RAPTORQ           = 0b0100_0000_0000;  // RaptorQ encoded (default)
        const CONTROL_PLANE     = 0b1000_0000_0000;  // Control plane object
    }
}
```

This issue owns **bit-level parsing/serialization** of flags; semantic handling (acks, streaming, control-plane routing) owned by MeshNode/FCPC.

---

## SymbolEnvelope (Full NORMATIVE Struct)

```rust
/// Full symbol envelope with encryption (NORMATIVE)
///
/// The universal transmission unit for FCP2.
/// All mesh objects are distributed as collections of SymbolEnvelopes.
pub struct SymbolEnvelope {
    /// Content address of complete object
    pub object_id: ObjectId,

    /// Encoding Symbol ID (position in fountain code)
    pub esi: u32,

    /// Source symbols needed (K)
    pub k: u16,

    /// Symbol payload (encrypted)
    pub data: Vec<u8>,

    /// Zone for key derivation
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,

    /// Zone key ID (for key rotation - enables deterministic decryption)
    /// Receivers lookup the correct zone key by this ID.
    pub zone_key_id: [u8; 8],

    /// Epoch for replay protection and temporal binding
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,

    /// Sender node id (NORMATIVE for per-sender subkeys)
    pub source_id: TailscaleNodeId,

    /// Sender instance ID (NORMATIVE for reboot safety)
    /// Random u64 generated at process startup. If sender restarts and frame_seq resets,
    /// the sender subkey changes because sender_instance_id differs.
    pub sender_instance_id: u64,

    /// Per-sender monotonic frame sequence (NORMATIVE)
    /// Monotonicity scope is (zone_id, zone_key_id, source_id, sender_instance_id).
    pub frame_seq: u64,

    /// AEAD authentication tag
    pub auth_tag: [u8; 16],

    // NOTE: Nonce is NOT stored per-symbol.
    // NORMATIVE: nonce = frame_seq_le || esi_le
    // frame_seq is per-sender monotonic for a given (zone_id, zone_key_id, source_id, sender_instance_id).
}
```

## Per-Symbol AEAD (NORMATIVE)

Security goal: deterministic, replay-resistant, reboot-safe encryption with bounded parsing.

### Per-Sender Subkey Derivation

```rust
/// Derive per-sender subkey from zone symmetric key (NORMATIVE)
///
/// This eliminates cross-sender nonce collision risk.
pub fn derive_sender_subkey(
    zone_symmetric_key: &[u8; 32],
    zone_key_id: &[u8; 8],
    sender_node_id: &TailscaleNodeId,
    sender_instance_id: u64,
) -> [u8; 32] {
    let mut info = Vec::new();
    info.extend_from_slice(b"FCP2-SENDER-KEY-V1");
    info.extend_from_slice(sender_node_id.as_bytes());
    info.extend_from_slice(&sender_instance_id.to_le_bytes());

    hkdf_sha256(
        zone_symmetric_key,
        zone_key_id,
        &info,
    )
}
```

### Deterministic Nonce Derivation (NORMATIVE)

```rust
/// Derive AEAD nonce deterministically (NORMATIVE).
///
/// - ChaCha20-Poly1305 (12-byte): nonce12 = frame_seq_le || esi_le
/// - XChaCha20-Poly1305 (24-byte): nonce24 = sender_instance_id_le || frame_seq_le || esi_le || 0u32
fn derive_nonce12(frame_seq: u64, esi: u32) -> [u8; 12] {
    let mut nonce = [0u8; 12];
    nonce[0..8].copy_from_slice(&frame_seq.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[8..12].copy_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());
    nonce
}

fn derive_nonce24(sender_instance_id: u64, frame_seq: u64, esi: u32) -> [u8; 24] {
    let mut nonce = [0u8; 24];
    nonce[0..8].copy_from_slice(&sender_instance_id.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[8..16].copy_from_slice(&frame_seq.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[16..20].copy_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[20..24].copy_from_slice(&0u32.to_le_bytes());
    nonce
}
```

**Why this design:**
- 64-bit random nonces have birthday collision risk over long-running systems
- Per-sender subkeys eliminate cross-sender nonce collision (keys differ per sender)
- `sender_instance_id` makes subkeys reboot-safe: if sender restarts and frame_seq resets to 0, new instance_id yields different subkey
- Deterministic nonces avoid RNG dependence and are testable
- No per-frame random generation overhead

### AAD Binding (NORMATIVE)

Associated data binds symbol to context (MUST match spec exactly):

```rust
impl SymbolEnvelope {
    /// Build AEAD associated data (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Fixed-size AAD for interop stability and DoS resistance.
    fn build_aad(
        object_id: &ObjectId,
        esi: u32,
        k: u16,
        zone_id: &ZoneId,
        zone_key_id: [u8; 8],
        epoch: EpochId,
    ) -> Vec<u8> {
        let mut aad = Vec::with_capacity(96);
        aad.extend_from_slice(object_id.as_bytes());     // 32 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());       // 4 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&k.to_le_bytes());         // 2 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(zone_id.hash().as_bytes()); // 32 bytes (ZoneIdHash)
        aad.extend_from_slice(&zone_key_id);             // 8 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&epoch.0.to_le_bytes());   // 8 bytes
        aad  // Total: 86 bytes
    }
}
```

**Important:** `sender_instance_id` and `frame_seq` are already bound cryptographically via the deterministic nonce and per-sender subkey derivation. They are NOT part of the SymbolEnvelope AAD.

### Encrypt/Decrypt Implementation

```rust
impl SymbolEnvelope {
    /// Encrypt symbol data with zone key (NORMATIVE)
    pub fn encrypt(
        object_id: ObjectId,
        esi: u32,
        k: u16,
        plaintext: &[u8],
        zone_key: &ZoneKey,
        epoch: EpochId,
        source_id: TailscaleNodeId,
        sender_instance_id: u64,
        frame_seq: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        let aad = Self::build_aad(&object_id, esi, k, &zone_key.zone_id, zone_key.key_id, epoch);
        let sender_key = zone_key.derive_sender_subkey(&source_id, sender_instance_id);

        let (ciphertext, auth_tag) = match zone_key.algorithm {
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::ChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce12(frame_seq, esi);
                zone_key.encrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, plaintext, &nonce, &aad)
            }
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::XChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce24(sender_instance_id, frame_seq, esi);
                zone_key.encrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, plaintext, &nonce, &aad)
            }
        };

        Self {
            object_id,
            esi,
            k,
            data: ciphertext,
            zone_id: zone_key.zone_id.clone(),
            zone_key_id: zone_key.key_id,
            epoch_id: epoch,
            source_id,
            sender_instance_id,
            frame_seq,
            auth_tag,
        }
    }

    /// Decrypt and verify symbol (NORMATIVE)
    pub fn decrypt(&self, zone_key: &ZoneKey) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError> {
        // Verify key_id matches to catch rotation mismatches early
        if zone_key.key_id != self.zone_key_id {
            return Err(CryptoError::KeyIdMismatch {
                expected: zone_key.key_id,
                got: self.zone_key_id,
            });
        }

        let aad = Self::build_aad(
            &self.object_id,
            self.esi,
            self.k,
            &self.zone_id,
            self.zone_key_id,
            self.epoch_id
        );

        let sender_key = zone_key.derive_sender_subkey(&self.source_id, self.sender_instance_id);
        match zone_key.algorithm {
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::ChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce12(self.frame_seq, self.esi);
                zone_key.decrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, &self.data, &nonce, &self.auth_tag, &aad)
            }
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::XChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce24(self.sender_instance_id, self.frame_seq, self.esi);
                zone_key.decrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, &self.data, &nonce, &self.auth_tag, &aad)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## DecodeStatus (NORMATIVE)

Enables flow control: receiver tells sender how many symbols received/needed.

```rust
/// Decode status feedback (NORMATIVE)
pub struct DecodeStatus {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub object_id: ObjectId,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,

}
```

**Anti-Amplification Rule (NORMATIVE):**
MeshNodes MUST NOT send more than N symbols in response to a request unless:
1. The requester is authenticated (session MAC or node signature), AND
2. The request includes a bounded missing-hint (e.g., `DecodeStatus.missing_hint`) or comparable proof-of-need

## Strict Bounds / DoS Resistance (MUST)

- Reject frames where computed lengths do not match `total_payload_len`
- Reject frames/datagrams exceeding configured MTU/bounds
- Avoid unbounded allocations for malformed `symbol_count` or `symbol_size`
- Symbol count MUST be validated: `symbol_count * (22 + symbol_size) == total_payload_len`

```rust
/// Validate FCPS frame (NORMATIVE)
fn validate_fcps_frame(frame: &[u8], max_datagram_bytes: usize) -> Result<(), FrameError> {
    if frame.len() > max_datagram_bytes {
        return Err(FrameError::ExceedsMtu { len: frame.len(), max: max_datagram_bytes });
    }
    if frame.len() < 114 {
        return Err(FrameError::TooShort { len: frame.len(), min: 114 });
    }

    let symbol_count = u32::from_le_bytes(frame[8..12].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;
    let total_payload_len = u32::from_le_bytes(frame[12..16].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;
    let symbol_size = u16::from_le_bytes(frame[48..50].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;

    let expected_payload = symbol_count * (4 + 2 + symbol_size + 16);
    if total_payload_len != expected_payload {
        return Err(FrameError::LengthMismatch {
            claimed: total_payload_len,
            computed: expected_payload
        });
    }
    if 114 + total_payload_len != frame.len() {
        return Err(FrameError::FrameSizeMismatch);
    }

    Ok(())
}
```

## Goal

This issue owns **byte-accurate encoding/decoding** of:

## On-Wire Datagram Envelope (NORMATIVE)

FCPS frames are carried inside an authenticated datagram envelope bound to a MeshSession:

```
FCPS_DATAGRAM (on-wire format):
  Bytes 0-15:   session_id [16]
  Bytes 16-23:  seq (u64 LE)
  Bytes 24-39:  mac [16] (Suite1/Suite2; truncated to 16 bytes)
  Bytes 40..:   fcps_frame_bytes (exact FCPS frame bytes per section 4.3)
```

### MAC Computation (NORMATIVE)

```rust
/// Compute session MAC for FCPS datagram (NORMATIVE)
fn compute_session_mac(
    k_mac_dir: &[u8; 32],
    session_id: &[u8; 16],
    direction: u8,  // 0x00 = i2r, 0x01 = r2i
    seq: u64,
    fcps_frame_bytes: &[u8],
) -> [u8; 16] {
    // Suite1: HMAC-SHA256 truncated to 16 bytes
    // Suite2: BLAKE3 keyed, truncated to 16 bytes
    let mut mac_input = Vec::new();
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(session_id);
    mac_input.push(direction);
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(&seq.to_le_bytes());
    mac_input.extend_from_slice(fcps_frame_bytes);

    let full_mac = blake3::keyed_hash(k_mac_dir, &mac_input);
    let mut truncated = [0u8; 16];
    truncated.copy_from_slice(&full_mac.as_bytes()[..16]);
    truncated
}
```

**Direction byte:**
- `0x00` for initiator to responder
- `0x01` for responder to initiator

### MTU Rule (NORMATIVE)

- `len(FCPS_DATAGRAM)` MUST be at most `max_datagram_bytes` (default: 1200)
- Applies to **full UDP payload** (envelope + frame), not just inner FCPS frame
- Implementations MUST reject datagrams exceeding configured maximum

## FCPS Frame Format (NORMATIVE; Fixed 114-byte Header)

```
FCPS FRAME FORMAT (Symbol-Native)

  Bytes 0-3:    Magic (0x46 0x43 0x50 0x53 = "FCPS")
  Bytes 4-5:    Version (u16 LE)
  Bytes 6-7:    Flags (u16 LE)
  Bytes 8-11:   Symbol Count (u32 LE)
  Bytes 12-15:  Total Payload Length (u32 LE)
  Bytes 16-47:  Object ID (32 bytes)
  Bytes 48-49:  Symbol Size (u16 LE, default 1024)
  Bytes 50-57:  Zone Key ID (8 bytes, for rotation)
  Bytes 58-89:  Zone ID hash (32 bytes, BLAKE3; see section 3.4)
  Bytes 90-97:  Epoch ID (u64 LE)
  Bytes 98-105: Sender Instance ID (u64 LE, reboot-safety for nonces)
  Bytes 106-113: Frame Seq (u64 LE, per-sender monotonic)
  Bytes 114+:   Symbol payloads (concatenated)

  Fixed header: 114 bytes
  Each symbol: 4 (ESI) + 2 (K) + N (data) + 16 (auth_tag)
               (nonce derived per algorithm; see derive_nonce12/24)

  NOTE (NORMATIVE): No separate checksum. Frame integrity is guaranteed by:
    - per-symbol AEAD auth tags (content-plane)
    - MeshSession MAC (data-plane, see section 4.2)
```

### Symbol Record Format (NORMATIVE)

Each symbol record within a frame:

```rust
/// Symbol record layout (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Each symbol record is:
/// - esi: u32 LE (4 bytes)     - Encoding Symbol ID
/// - k: u16 LE (2 bytes)       - Total source symbols
/// - data: [u8; N]             - Encrypted symbol payload
/// - auth_tag: [u8; 16]        - AEAD authentication tag
///
/// Total: 4 + 2 + symbol_size + 16 = 22 + symbol_size bytes per symbol
pub struct SymbolRecord {
    pub esi: u32,
    pub k: u16,
    pub data: Vec<u8>,
    pub auth_tag: [u8; 16],
}
```

## Per-Symbol AEAD (NORMATIVE)

Security goal: deterministic, replay-resistant, reboot-safe encryption with bounded parsing.

### Per-Sender Subkey Derivation

```rust
/// Derive per-sender subkey from zone symmetric key (NORMATIVE)
///
/// This eliminates cross-sender nonce collision risk.
pub fn derive_sender_subkey(
    zone_symmetric_key: &[u8; 32],
    zone_key_id: &[u8; 8],
    sender_node_id: &TailscaleNodeId,
    sender_instance_id: u64,
) -> [u8; 32] {
    let mut info = Vec::new();
    info.extend_from_slice(b"FCP2-SENDER-KEY-V1");
    info.extend_from_slice(sender_node_id.as_bytes());
    info.extend_from_slice(&sender_instance_id.to_le_bytes());

    hkdf_sha256(
        zone_symmetric_key,
        zone_key_id,
        &info,
    )
}
```

### Deterministic Nonce Derivation (NORMATIVE)

```rust
/// Derive AEAD nonce deterministically (NORMATIVE).
///
/// - ChaCha20-Poly1305 (12-byte): nonce12 = frame_seq_le || esi_le
/// - XChaCha20-Poly1305 (24-byte): nonce24 = sender_instance_id_le || frame_seq_le || esi_le || 0u32
fn derive_nonce12(frame_seq: u64, esi: u32) -> [u8; 12] {
    let mut nonce = [0u8; 12];
    nonce[0..8].copy_from_slice(&frame_seq.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[8..12].copy_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());
    nonce
}

fn derive_nonce24(sender_instance_id: u64, frame_seq: u64, esi: u32) -> [u8; 24] {
    let mut nonce = [0u8; 24];
    nonce[0..8].copy_from_slice(&sender_instance_id.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[8..16].copy_from_slice(&frame_seq.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[16..20].copy_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());
    nonce[20..24].copy_from_slice(&0u32.to_le_bytes());
    nonce
}
```

**Why this design:**
- 64-bit random nonces have birthday collision risk over long-running systems
- Per-sender subkeys eliminate cross-sender nonce collision (keys differ per sender)
- `sender_instance_id` makes subkeys reboot-safe: if sender restarts and frame_seq resets to 0, new instance_id yields different subkey
- Deterministic nonces avoid RNG dependence and are testable
- No per-frame random generation overhead

### AAD Binding (NORMATIVE)

Associated data binds symbol to context (MUST match spec exactly):

```rust
impl SymbolEnvelope {
    /// Build AEAD associated data (NORMATIVE)
    ///
    /// Fixed-size AAD for interop stability and DoS resistance.
    fn build_aad(
        object_id: &ObjectId,
        esi: u32,
        k: u16,
        zone_id: &ZoneId,
        zone_key_id: [u8; 8],
        epoch: EpochId,
    ) -> Vec<u8> {
        let mut aad = Vec::with_capacity(96);
        aad.extend_from_slice(object_id.as_bytes());     // 32 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&esi.to_le_bytes());       // 4 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&k.to_le_bytes());         // 2 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(zone_id.hash().as_bytes()); // 32 bytes (ZoneIdHash)
        aad.extend_from_slice(&zone_key_id);             // 8 bytes
        aad.extend_from_slice(&epoch.0.to_le_bytes());   // 8 bytes
        aad  // Total: 86 bytes
    }
}
```

**Important:** `sender_instance_id` and `frame_seq` are already bound cryptographically via the deterministic nonce and per-sender subkey derivation. They are NOT part of the SymbolEnvelope AAD.

### Encrypt/Decrypt Implementation

```rust
impl SymbolEnvelope {
    /// Encrypt symbol data with zone key (NORMATIVE)
    pub fn encrypt(
        object_id: ObjectId,
        esi: u32,
        k: u16,
        plaintext: &[u8],
        zone_key: &ZoneKey,
        epoch: EpochId,
        source_id: TailscaleNodeId,
        sender_instance_id: u64,
        frame_seq: u64,
    ) -> Self {
        let aad = Self::build_aad(&object_id, esi, k, &zone_key.zone_id, zone_key.key_id, epoch);
        let sender_key = zone_key.derive_sender_subkey(&source_id, sender_instance_id);

        let (ciphertext, auth_tag) = match zone_key.algorithm {
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::ChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce12(frame_seq, esi);
                zone_key.encrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, plaintext, &nonce, &aad)
            }
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::XChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce24(sender_instance_id, frame_seq, esi);
                zone_key.encrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, plaintext, &nonce, &aad)
            }
        };

        Self {
            object_id,
            esi,
            k,
            data: ciphertext,
            zone_id: zone_key.zone_id.clone(),
            zone_key_id: zone_key.key_id,
            epoch_id: epoch,
            source_id,
            sender_instance_id,
            frame_seq,
            auth_tag,
        }
    }

    /// Decrypt and verify symbol (NORMATIVE)
    pub fn decrypt(&self, zone_key: &ZoneKey) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError> {
        // Verify key_id matches to catch rotation mismatches early
        if zone_key.key_id != self.zone_key_id {
            return Err(CryptoError::KeyIdMismatch {
                expected: zone_key.key_id,
                got: self.zone_key_id,
            });
        }

        let aad = Self::build_aad(
            &self.object_id,
            self.esi,
            self.k,
            &self.zone_id,
            self.zone_key_id,
            self.epoch_id
        );

        let sender_key = zone_key.derive_sender_subkey(&self.source_id, self.sender_instance_id);
        match zone_key.algorithm {
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::ChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce12(self.frame_seq, self.esi);
                zone_key.decrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, &self.data, &nonce, &self.auth_tag, &aad)
            }
            ZoneKeyAlgorithm::XChaCha20Poly1305 => {
                let nonce = derive_nonce24(self.sender_instance_id, self.frame_seq, self.esi);
                zone_key.decrypt_with_subkey(&sender_key, &self.data, &nonce, &self.auth_tag, &aad)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## DecodeStatus (NORMATIVE)

Enables flow control: receiver tells sender how many symbols received/needed.

```rust
/// Decode status feedback (NORMATIVE)
pub struct DecodeStatus {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub object_id: ObjectId,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    pub zone_key_id: [u8; 8],
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,
    /// Unique symbols received so far for this object
    pub received_unique: u32,
    /// Target required to decode (K-prime)
    /// K-prime is approximately K times 1.002 for RaptorQ
    pub needed: u32,
    /// Success flag
    pub complete: bool,
    /// Optional: hint about missing ESIs for targeted repair
    /// MUST be bounded (max 100 entries recommended)
    pub missing_hint: Option<Vec<u32>>,
    /// Signature by receiving node
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

**Anti-Amplification Rule (NORMATIVE):**
MeshNodes MUST NOT send more than N symbols in response to a request unless:
1. The requester is authenticated (session MAC or node signature), AND
2. The request includes a bounded missing-hint (e.g., `DecodeStatus.missing_hint`) or comparable proof-of-need

---

## Strict Bounds / DoS Resistance (MUST)

- Reject frames where computed lengths do not match `total_payload_len`
- Reject frames/datagrams exceeding configured MTU/bounds
- Avoid unbounded allocations for malformed `symbol_count` or `symbol_size`
- Symbol count MUST be validated: `symbol_count * (22 + symbol_size) == total_payload_len`

```rust
/// Validate FCPS frame (NORMATIVE)
fn validate_fcps_frame(frame: &[u8], max_datagram_bytes: usize) -> Result<(), FrameError> {
    if frame.len() > max_datagram_bytes {
        return Err(FrameError::ExceedsMtu { len: frame.len(), max: max_datagram_bytes });
    }
    if frame.len() < 114 {
        return Err(FrameError::TooShort { len: frame.len(), min: 114 });
    }

    let symbol_count = u32::from_le_bytes(frame[8..12].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;
    let total_payload_len = u32::from_le_bytes(frame[12..16].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;
    let symbol_size = u16::from_le_bytes(frame[48..50].try_into().unwrap()) as usize;

    let expected_payload = symbol_count * (4 + 2 + symbol_size + 16);
    if total_payload_len != expected_payload {
        return Err(FrameError::LengthMismatch {
            claimed: total_payload_len,
            computed: expected_payload
        });
    }
    if 114 + total_payload_len != frame.len() {
        return Err(FrameError::FrameSizeMismatch);
    }

    Ok(())
}
```

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.13","title":"[FCP2] Wire Protocol (Control Plane): FCPC Frames + Canonical ControlPlaneObject Retention","description":"# [FCP2] Wire Protocol (Control Plane): FCPC Frames + Canonical ControlPlaneObject Retention

## Goal
Implement reliable, ordered, backpressured control-plane framing for canonical objects (invoke/response/receipts/approvals/audit/etc).

FCPC exists to make small control-plane messages fast and reliable without forcing everything through RaptorQ.

## Normative transport requirements
- Implementations **MUST** support FCPC over **QUIC streams** inside the tailnet.
- Implementations **MAY** support FCPC over TCP as a fallback.

## Security requirements (NORMATIVE)
- FCPC messages MUST be bound to an authenticated MeshSession.
- FCPC payloads MUST be authenticated using `k_ctx` derived from the MeshSession key schedule.
- Implementations SHOULD encrypt FCPC payloads (AEAD) using `k_ctx`.

## Control-plane object model (NORMATIVE)
All control-plane message types MUST have a canonical CBOR object representation with SchemaId/ObjectId.

### ControlPlaneObject
- `ObjectHeader`
- `body` = canonical payload bytes (`schema_hash || canonical_cbor`)

### Retention classes (NORMATIVE)
Some control-plane objects MUST be stored (auditable/replayable), others MAY be ephemeral.

Examples:
- **Required**: invoke/response, receipts, approvals, secret access, revocations, audit events/heads
- **Ephemeral**: health, handshake objects, decode_status, symbol_ack, introspect, configure, simulate

## FCPC frame format (NORMATIVE)
Conceptual frame:

```
magic = "FCPC"
version = u16
session_id = [16]
seq = u64 (per-direction monotonic)
flags = u16
len = u32
ciphertext[len] (AEAD under k_ctx)
tag = [16]
```

AEAD rules:
- Nonce derived deterministically from `seq` (direction-specific).
- AAD includes at minimum `session_id || seq || flags`.

Replay protection:
- Receivers MUST enforce a bounded replay/reorder window (same scale as SessionReplayPolicy.max_reorder_window).
- `seq` MUST be strictly increasing per direction for an authenticated session.

## Transport options (spec surface)
The spec describes multiple ways to transport control-plane objects:
1. **FCPC (required)**: QUIC stream framing using `k_ctx`
2. **Direct local (optional)**: canonical bytes over local connector transport (may reuse FCPC framing)
3. **Mesh fallback (optional but important)**: encode canonical ControlPlaneObjects to symbols and send inside FCPS frames with `FrameFlags::CONTROL_PLANE` set

This issue owns (1) and a reusable framing library. (3) is implemented/owned by MeshNode because it couples to routing, stores, and repair.

## Deliverables
- `FcpcFrame` encoder/decoder with strict bounds checks and no unbounded allocation.
- Backpressure/flow control integration with QUIC stream semantics.
- Retention enforcement hooks: Required objects are stored; Ephemeral objects may be dropped after processing.

## Tests
- Unit tests:
  - seq ordering
  - replay window enforcement
  - backpressure behavior under slow consumers
- Fuzz target for FCPC frame parsing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FCPC can carry InvokeRequest/Response + receipts end-to-end in the system harness.
- Conformance fuzz target runs without panics.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.14","title":"[FCP2] RaptorQ + Chunking: Symbol Encoding/Decoding, ChunkedObjectManifest, Decode DoS Mitigation","description":"# [FCP2] RaptorQ + Chunking: Symbol Encoding/Decoding, ChunkedObjectManifest, Decode DoS Mitigation

## Goal
Implement symbol encoding/decoding for mesh objects and large payload handling via RaptorQ fountain codes and chunked object manifests.

## Background & Rationale

### Why RaptorQ Fountain Codes

FCP2 uses RaptorQ as the universal encoding scheme because:

- **Any K' symbols reconstruct the original** (K' approximately K times 1.002)
- **No symbol is special** - all are equally useful for reconstruction
- **No coordination needed** - receivers don't need to coordinate which symbols they receive
- **Lost symbols don't require retransmission** - any new symbol helps
- **Multipath aggregation** - symbols from any source contribute equally
- **DoS resistant** - no single symbol is critical
- **Resumable without bookmarks** - just accumulate more symbols

### Why Chunking for Large Objects

RaptorQ is great for "any K' symbols reconstruct the whole object", but it forces all-or-nothing reconstruction and can cause memory spikes for large objects (binaries, attachments, big audit epochs).

Chunking enables:
- **Partial retrieval** (first chunks first for streaming)
- **Targeted repair** (repair the missing chunk, not the whole multi-MB object)
- **Dedupe across versions** (chunk-level content addressing)
- **Bounded memory** (reconstruct one chunk at a time)
- **Smoother streaming** (progressive display)

---

## Normative Configuration

```rust
/// RaptorQ configuration (NORMATIVE)
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct RaptorQConfig {
    /// Symbol size in bytes
    /// Default: 1024
    pub symbol_size: u16,

    /// Repair ratio in basis points (NORMATIVE)
    /// 500 = 5% = K * 1.05 total symbols
    /// Default: 500
    pub repair_ratio_bps: u16,

    /// Maximum object size that can be encoded
    /// Default: 64MB
    pub max_object_size: u32,

    /// Maximum time to wait for object reconstruction
    /// Default: 30s
    pub decode_timeout: Duration,

    /// Objects above this size MUST use ChunkedObjectManifest
    /// Default: 256KB
    pub max_chunk_threshold: u32,

    /// Chunk size for ChunkedObjectManifest
    /// Default: 64KB
    pub chunk_size: u32,
}

impl Default for RaptorQConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            symbol_size: 1024,
            repair_ratio_bps: 500,
            max_object_size: 64 * 1024 * 1024, // 64MB
            decode_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
            max_chunk_threshold: 256 * 1024, // 256KB
            chunk_size: 64 * 1024, // 64KB
        }
    }
}

impl RaptorQConfig {
    /// Calculate number of repair symbols from basis points
    ///
    /// repair_ratio_bps = 500 means 5% overhead
    /// For K source symbols, generate K + K*500/10000 = K*1.05 total symbols
    pub fn repair_symbols(&self, source_symbols: u32) -> u32 {
        (source_symbols as u64 * self.repair_ratio_bps as u64 / 10000) as u32
    }

    /// Calculate K (source symbols) needed for a payload
    pub fn source_symbols(&self, payload_len: usize) -> u32 {
        ((payload_len + self.symbol_size as usize - 1) / self.symbol_size as usize) as u32
    }
}
```

## ChunkedObjectManifest (NORMATIVE)

Large objects above `max_chunk_threshold` MUST be represented as a manifest referencing ordered `RawChunk` objects.

```rust
/// Chunked object manifest (NORMATIVE for objects above max_chunk_threshold)
///
/// Enables:
/// - Partial retrieval (fetch chunks on demand)
/// - Targeted repair (repair one chunk, not whole object)
/// - Bounded memory reconstruction
/// - Chunk-level deduplication
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ChunkedObjectManifest {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// Total byte length of the original payload
    pub total_len: u64,
    /// Chunk size in bytes (except possibly last chunk)
    pub chunk_size: u32,
    /// Ordered chunk object IDs (each chunk is a normal StoredObject)
    pub chunks: Vec<ObjectId>,
    /// BLAKE3 hash of the full payload for end-to-end verification
    pub payload_hash: [u8; 32],
}

impl ChunkedObjectManifest {
    /// Create a manifest from a large payload
    pub fn from_payload(
        payload: &[u8],
        zone_id: &ZoneId,
        object_id_key: &ObjectIdKey,
        chunk_size: u32,
    ) -> (Self, Vec<RawChunk>) {
        let payload_hash = blake3::hash(payload);
        let mut chunks = Vec::new();
        let mut chunk_ids = Vec::new();

        for chunk_data in payload.chunks(chunk_size as usize) {
            let chunk = RawChunk::new(chunk_data.to_vec(), zone_id);
            let chunk_id = chunk.derive_object_id(zone_id, object_id_key);
            chunk_ids.push(chunk_id);
            chunks.push(chunk);
        }

        let manifest = Self {
            header: ObjectHeader::new(
                SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "ChunkedObjectManifest", Version::new(2, 0, 0)),
                zone_id.clone(),
            ),
            total_len: payload.len() as u64,
            chunk_size,
            chunks: chunk_ids,
            payload_hash: *payload_hash.as_bytes(),
        };

        (manifest, chunks)
    }

    /// Reconstruct the payload from chunks (validates hash)
    pub fn reconstruct(&self, chunks: &[RawChunk]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ChunkError> {
        if chunks.len() != self.chunks.len() {
            return Err(ChunkError::MissingChunks {
                expected: self.chunks.len(),
                got: chunks.len(),
            });
        }

        let mut payload = Vec::with_capacity(self.total_len as usize);
        for chunk in chunks {
            payload.extend_from_slice(&chunk.bytes);
        }

        // Verify total length
        if payload.len() as u64 != self.total_len {
            return Err(ChunkError::LengthMismatch {
                expected: self.total_len,
                got: payload.len() as u64,
            });
        }

        // Verify hash
        let actual_hash = blake3::hash(&payload);
        if actual_hash.as_bytes() != &self.payload_hash {
            return Err(ChunkError::HashMismatch);
        }

        Ok(payload)
    }

    /// Number of chunks in the manifest
    pub fn chunk_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.chunks.len()
    }
}

/// A chunk is a normal object with raw bytes body (NORMATIVE)
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct RawChunk {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub bytes: Vec<u8>,
}

impl RawChunk {
    pub fn new(bytes: Vec<u8>, zone_id: &ZoneId) -> Self {
        Self {
            header: ObjectHeader::new(
                SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "RawChunk", Version::new(2, 0, 0)),
                zone_id.clone(),
            ),
            bytes,
        }
    }

    pub fn derive_object_id(&self, zone_id: &ZoneId, key: &ObjectIdKey) -> ObjectId {
        let schema = &self.header.schema;
        let content = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(self, schema);
        ObjectId::new(&content, zone_id, schema, key)
    }
}

/// Chunk reconstruction errors
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum ChunkError {
    MissingChunks { expected: usize, got: usize },
    LengthMismatch { expected: u64, got: u64 },
    HashMismatch,
}
```

## RaptorQ Encoder/Decoder Interface

```rust
/// RaptorQ encoder for producing symbols from a payload
pub struct RaptorQEncoder {
    inner: raptorq::Encoder,
    config: RaptorQConfig,
    object_id: ObjectId,
}

impl RaptorQEncoder {
    /// Create encoder for a payload
    pub fn new(payload: &[u8], object_id: ObjectId, config: &RaptorQConfig) -> Result<Self, EncodeError> {
        if payload.len() > config.max_object_size as usize {
            return Err(EncodeError::PayloadTooLarge {
                size: payload.len(),
                max: config.max_object_size as usize,
            });
        }

        let inner = raptorq::Encoder::with_defaults(payload, config.symbol_size);
        Ok(Self {
            inner,
            config: config.clone(),
            object_id,
        })
    }

    /// Get K (number of source symbols)
    pub fn source_symbols(&self) -> u32 {
        self.inner.get_config().source_blocks().iter()
            .map(|sb| sb.symbols())
            .sum()
    }

    /// Generate all source + repair symbols
    ///
    /// Returns iterator over (esi, symbol_data)
    pub fn encode_all(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = (u32, Vec<u8>)> + '_ {
        let k = self.source_symbols();
        let repair = self.config.repair_symbols(k);
        let total = k + repair;

        (0..total).map(move |esi| {
            let packet = self.inner.get_encoded_packet(esi as u64);
            (esi, packet.data().to_vec())
        })
    }

    /// Generate a specific symbol by ESI
    pub fn encode_symbol(&self, esi: u32) -> Vec<u8> {
        self.inner.get_encoded_packet(esi as u64).data().to_vec()
    }
}

/// RaptorQ decoder for reconstructing payload from symbols
pub struct RaptorQDecoder {
    inner: raptorq::Decoder,
    config: RaptorQConfig,
    received: HashSet<u32>,
    k: u32,
    started_at: Instant,
}

impl RaptorQDecoder {
    /// Create decoder with expected K symbols
    pub fn new(k: u32, config: &RaptorQConfig) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner: raptorq::Decoder::new(raptorq::ObjectTransmissionInformation::with_defaults(
                0, // Will be determined by first symbol
                config.symbol_size,
            )),
            config: config.clone(),
            received: HashSet::new(),
            k,
            started_at: Instant::now(),
        }
    }

    /// Add a symbol and attempt reconstruction
    ///
    /// Returns Some(payload) when reconstruction succeeds
    pub fn add_symbol(&mut self, esi: u32, data: Vec<u8>) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, DecodeError> {
        // Check timeout
        if self.started_at.elapsed() > self.config.decode_timeout {
            return Err(DecodeError::Timeout);
        }

        // Skip duplicates
        if self.received.contains(&esi) {
            return Ok(None);
        }

        self.received.insert(esi);

        // Try decode
        let packet = raptorq::EncodingPacket::new(
            raptorq::PayloadId::new(0, esi as u64),
            data,
        );

        match self.inner.decode(packet) {
            Some(payload) => Ok(Some(payload)),
            None => Ok(None),
        }
    }

    /// Number of unique symbols received
    pub fn received_count(&self) -> u32 {
        self.received.len() as u32
    }

    /// Approximate number needed for reconstruction
    /// K' is approximately K * 1.002
    pub fn needed(&self) -> u32 {
        (self.k as f64 * 1.002).ceil() as u32
    }

    /// Check if we likely have enough symbols
    pub fn likely_complete(&self) -> bool {
        self.received_count() >= self.needed()
    }
}

/// Encode errors
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum EncodeError {
    PayloadTooLarge { size: usize, max: usize },
}

/// Decode errors
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum DecodeError {
    Timeout,
    InsufficientSymbols { received: u32, needed: u32 },
}
```

## Decode DoS Mitigation (NORMATIVE)

Decoding MUST be bounded to prevent resource exhaustion attacks:

```rust
/// Decode admission controller (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Prevents resource exhaustion from decode DoS attacks by:
/// - Bounding concurrent decodes
/// - Enforcing timeouts
/// - Limiting allocation per decode
/// - Prioritizing pinned/referenced objects over unknown
pub struct DecodeAdmissionController {
    /// Maximum concurrent decode operations
    max_concurrent: usize,
    /// Current active decodes
    active: Arc<AtomicUsize>,
    /// Per-decode memory limit
    max_memory_per_decode: usize,
    /// Decode timeout
    timeout: Duration,
    /// Symbol buffer limit per object
    max_symbols_buffered: u32,
}

impl DecodeAdmissionController {
    pub fn new(config: &RaptorQConfig) -> Self {
        Self {
            max_concurrent: 16, // Configurable
            active: Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0)),
            max_memory_per_decode: config.max_object_size as usize,
            timeout: config.decode_timeout,
            max_symbols_buffered: 10000, // Reasonable bound
        }
    }

    /// Try to acquire a decode slot
    pub fn try_acquire(&self) -> Option<DecodePermit> {
        let current = self.active.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
        if current >= self.max_concurrent {
            self.active.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            return None;
        }
        Some(DecodePermit {
            active: self.active.clone(),
            started_at: Instant::now(),
            timeout: self.timeout,
            max_memory: self.max_memory_per_decode,
            max_symbols: self.max_symbols_buffered,
            symbols_buffered: 0,
            memory_used: 0,
        })
    }
}

/// RAII permit for a decode operation
pub struct DecodePermit {
    active: Arc<AtomicUsize>,
    started_at: Instant,
    timeout: Duration,
    max_memory: usize,
    max_symbols: u32,
    symbols_buffered: u32,
    memory_used: usize,
}

impl DecodePermit {
    /// Check if permit is still valid (not timed out)
    pub fn is_valid(&self) -> bool {
        self.started_at.elapsed() < self.timeout
    }

    /// Try to buffer a symbol (checks limits)
    pub fn try_buffer_symbol(&mut self, symbol_size: usize) -> bool {
        if self.symbols_buffered >= self.max_symbols {
            return false;
        }
        if self.memory_used + symbol_size > self.max_memory {
            return false;
        }
        self.symbols_buffered += 1;
        self.memory_used += symbol_size;
        true
    }
}

impl Drop for DecodePermit {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        self.active.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
    }
}
```

## Object Encoding Decision (NORMATIVE)

```rust
/// Decide how to encode an object based on size
pub enum EncodingDecision {
    /// Small object: encode directly with RaptorQ
    Direct { symbols: Vec<(u32, Vec<u8>)> },
    /// Large object: use chunked manifest
    Chunked {
        manifest: ChunkedObjectManifest,
        chunks: Vec<RawChunk>,
    },
}

impl EncodingDecision {
    /// Decide encoding strategy for a payload
    pub fn for_payload(
        payload: &[u8],
        zone_id: &ZoneId,
        object_id_key: &ObjectIdKey,
        config: &RaptorQConfig,
    ) -> Result<Self, EncodeError> {
        if payload.len() > config.max_object_size as usize {
            return Err(EncodeError::PayloadTooLarge {
                size: payload.len(),
                max: config.max_object_size as usize,
            });
        }

        if payload.len() > config.max_chunk_threshold as usize {
            // Large object: use chunking
            let (manifest, chunks) = ChunkedObjectManifest::from_payload(
                payload,
                zone_id,
                object_id_key,
                config.chunk_size,
            );
            Ok(Self::Chunked { manifest, chunks })
        } else {
            // Small object: direct RaptorQ
            let object_id = ObjectId::new(
                payload,
                zone_id,
                &SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "RawObject", Version::new(2, 0, 0)),
                object_id_key,
            );
            let encoder = RaptorQEncoder::new(payload, object_id, config)?;
            let symbols: Vec<_> = encoder.encode_all().collect();
            Ok(Self::Direct { symbols })
        }
    }
}
```

## Epoch Event Buffer Integration

```rust
/// Epoch-based RaptorQ event buffer (NORMATIVE)
///
/// Events within an epoch are batched and encoded together.
pub struct RaptorQEventBuffer {
    epoch_duration: Duration,
    current_epoch: RwLock<EpochWriter>,
    finalized_epochs: RwLock<HashMap<u64, EpochMetadata>>,
    config: RaptorQConfig,
}

impl RaptorQEventBuffer {
    pub async fn finalize_epoch(&self) -> Result<EpochMetadata, EncodeError> {
        let epoch = self.current_epoch.write().await.take();
        let events_cbor = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(
            &epoch.events,
            &SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "EpochEvents", Version::new(2, 0, 0)),
        );
        let object_id = ObjectId::from_content(&events_cbor);

        // Encode to symbols and distribute
        let encoder = RaptorQEncoder::new(&events_cbor, object_id, &self.config)?;
        let symbols: Vec<_> = encoder.encode_all().collect();

        let metadata = EpochMetadata {
            epoch_id: epoch.id,
            object_id,
            symbol_count: symbols.len() as u32,
            k: encoder.source_symbols(),
            finalized_at: current_timestamp(),
        };

        // Store metadata
        self.finalized_epochs.write().await.insert(epoch.id, metadata.clone());

        // Distribute symbols to peers (handled by MeshNode)
        // self.distribute_symbols(epoch.id, symbols).await?;

        Ok(metadata)
    }
}

struct EpochWriter {
    id: u64,
    events: Vec<EventEnvelope>,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct EpochMetadata {
    pub epoch_id: u64,
    pub object_id: ObjectId,
    pub symbol_count: u32,
    pub k: u32,
    pub finalized_at: u64,
}
```

## Goal
Make offline resilience real by implementing:
- content-addressed object store (canonical objects)
- symbol store (partial availability)
- placement policies and measurable coverage
- periodic repair loop (targeted + rate-limited)
- retention classes and well-defined GC roots
- quarantine admission pipeline for untrusted/unreferenced objects

## Required behaviors (spec-aligned)
### Coverage is measurable
Nodes must quantify symbol/object availability using stable, interop-safe metrics (basis points):
- coverage ratio (bps)
- max concentration (avoid single-node dependency)
- distinct-node count

### Repair is bounded and convergent
- Repair loop must push coverage toward policy targets over time.
- Repair must be rate-limited and account for admission-control budgets.

### Retention + GC roots are explicit
- **Store retention** uses `RetentionClass` semantics for stored objects (Pinned | Lease{expires_at} | Ephemeral).
- **Control-plane retention** (Required vs Ephemeral) is a separate concept owned by FCPC/control-plane and maps to store retention by policy.
- GC roots include the zone’s pinned `ZoneCheckpoint` (and other pinned policy/config heads).

### Quarantine admission pipeline (NORMATIVE)
To prevent storage exhaustion from injected garbage ObjectIds:
- Unknown/unreferenced ObjectIds MUST be stored in a **bounded quarantine store** by default.
- Quarantined ObjectIds MUST NOT be inserted into global gossip filters/state until promoted.
- Promotion from quarantine → admitted is allowed only if:
  - object becomes reachable from the zone’s pinned `ZoneCheckpoint`, OR
  - object is explicitly requested by an authenticated peer via a bounded request, OR
  - object is explicitly pinned locally by user action/policy.
- Promotion MUST require successful reconstruction + schema verification (prevents admitting garbage as “real”).

## Subtasks
- Define ObjectPlacementPolicy and CoverageEvaluation (basis points for interop stability).
- Implement ObjectStore + SymbolStore interfaces with strict bounds.
- Implement RepairController run loop with rate limiting + targeted repair support.
- Define retention classes and GC roots.
- Implement quarantine store quotas (per-zone bytes/objects + TTL eviction) and promotion logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for placement evaluation + retention semantics.
- Simulation tests for repair loop improving coverage.
- Adversarial tests for quarantine quotas + promotion rules.
- Benchmarks for symbol store operations.
